messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following dental practice patient privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"Bright Smiles Dental collects your name, date of birth, contact details, medical history, and dental records to provide dental treatment. We process health data as necessary for the provision of healthcare under Art. 9(2)(h). Patient records are retained for 10 years after the last appointment as required by dental professional regulations. You have the right to access, correct, restrict, port, and erase your data, and to object to processing, by contacting privacy@brightsmiles.com. Our DPO is at dpo@brightsmiles.com. We use Dentally practice management software (UK-hosted) under a signed DPA. We notify the ICO within 72 hours of a breach."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Healthcare necessity (Art. 6(1)(c)) via dental professional regulations implied. Contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) also applicable for private dental treatment.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PASS] Health and dental records correctly identified as special category data. Art. 9(2)(h) (healthcare provision) explicitly cited as the processing basis.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Dental treatment provision is a clear and specific purpose.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Name, DOB, contact details, medical history, and dental records are proportionate and necessary for dental care.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 10-year post-last-appointment retention stated and grounded in dental professional regulatory requirements.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PASS] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), erasure (Art. 17), restriction (Art. 18), portability (Art. 20), and objection (Art. 21) all listed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Dentally named as practice management software provider with UK hosting and signed DPA.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] UK-hosted Dentally confirms no EEA transfer concerns.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact at dpo@brightsmiles.com disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour ICO notification stated.

VERDICT: Compliant
VIOLATIONS: None
RECOMMENDATIONS: No changes required. Consider explicitly stating Art. 6(1)(b) or (c) as the lawful basis alongside the Art. 9(2)(h) basis for additional transparency, and adding the right to lodge a complaint with the ICO (Art. 77).
